_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Fracture - Conservative Management - Without plaster PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Were the Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Was the X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs)submitted?
Was the post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) of affected part submitted?
Are the detailed procedure / operative Notes submitted?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advise submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or X-ray submitted for pre-authorization are not indicative of Fracture - Conservative Management - Without plaster.
Pre-procedure X-ray of affected part is missing or incorrectly labelled (patient ID, date, or side).
Post-procedure X-ray of affected part is missing or incorrectly labelled (patient ID, date, or side).
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary is missing or lacking follow-up advice.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
The fracture healing involves a combination of inflammatory, vascular, anabolic and catabolic events to allow eventual return of function.
